meta.title: VLSI Design Interview Questions | Teks Academy
meta.description: Explore top VLSI Design interview questions and answers covering ASIC, FPGA, Verilog, Semiconductor Design, RTL Design, and Embedded Systems.

heading: VLSI Design
Interview Questions
heroSection.subHeading: Prepare for VLSI Design interviews with semiconductor and hardware-focused interview questions and answers.

questions.question: What is VLSI?
questions.answer: VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) is the process of creating integrated circuits by combining thousands to millions of transistors on a single chip.

questions.question: What is the difference between ASIC and FPGA?
answer:  ASIC: Application-Specific Integrated Circuit, custom-designed for a particular use.
 FPGA: Field-Programmable Gate Array, programmable after manufacturing.

questions.question: What is Moore’s Law?
questions.answer: Moore’s Law states that the number of transistors in an IC doubles approximately every two years.

questions.question: What is the difference between combinational and sequential circuits?
answer:  Combinational: Output depends only on current inputs.
 Sequential: Output depends on current inputs and past states (uses memory elements).

questions.question: What is setup time and hold time?
answer:  Setup Time: Minimum time before the clock edge that data must be stable.
 Hold Time: Minimum time after the clock edge that data must remain stable.

questions.question: What is metastability?
questions.answer: A condition in flip-flops where the output is unpredictable due to violation of setup or hold time.

questions.question: What is RTL in VLSI?
questions.answer: Register Transfer Level – a design abstraction representing data flow between registers using hardware description languages.

questions.question: What is CMOS?
questions.answer: Complementary Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor – uses both NMOS and PMOS transistors for logic implementation.

questions.question: What are NMOS and PMOS?
answer:  NMOS: Active when gate voltage is high.
 PMOS: Active when gate voltage is low.

questions.question: What is the difference between latch and flip-flop?
What is fan-in and fan-out?
answer:  Fan-in: Number of inputs a gate can handle.
 Fan-out: Number of inputs a gate output can drive.

questions.question: What is propagation delay?
questions.answer: Time taken for the output of a gate to respond after the input changes.

questions.question: What is clock skew?
questions.answer: Difference in arrival time of the clock signal at different parts of the circuit.

questions.question: What is power gating?
questions.answer: Technique to reduce leakage power by turning off power to unused blocks.

questions.question: What is dynamic and static power consumption?
What is synthesis in VLSI?
questions.answer: Converting RTL code (Verilog/VHDL) into gate-level netlist.

questions.question: What is floorplanning?
questions.answer: Arranging blocks and macros on a chip before place and route.

questions.question: What is clock gating?
questions.answer: A technique to reduce dynamic power by disabling the clock in idle modules.

questions.question: What is IR drop?
questions.answer: Voltage drop due to resistance in power delivery network.

questions.question: What is electromigration?
questions.answer: Movement of metal atoms due to high current density causing circuit failure.

questions.question: What is a standard cell?
questions.answer: Pre-designed, reusable logic blocks used in ASIC design.

questions.question: What is DRC and LVS?
answer:  DRC: Design Rule Check – ensures layout follows fabrication rules.
 LVS: Layout vs tic – ensures layout matches circuit tic.

questions.question: What is a netlist?
questions.answer: A file containing components and their interconnections.

questions.question: What is hold violation and how to fix it?
questions.answer: A condition where data changes too soon after the clock edge. Fixed using delay buffers.

questions.question: What is setup violation and how to fix it?
questions.answer: Data arrives too late before the clock edge. Fixed by reducing logic delay or improving clock distribution.

questions.question: What is scan chain?
questions.answer: A chain of flip-flops used for testing by shifting in test patterns.

questions.question: What is ATPG?
questions.answer: Automatic Test Pattern Generation – used for generating test vectors for fault detection.

questions.question: What is BIST?
questions.answer: Built-In Self-Test – circuit can test itself using internal logic.

questions.question: What is timing analysis?
questions.answer: The process of checking whether the circuit meets setup and hold timing requirements.

questions.question: What is STA?
questions.answer: Static Timing Analysis – calculates worst-case timing without simulating all inputs.

questions.question: What is pipelining?
questions.answer: Splitting tasks into stages to improve throughput.

questions.question: What is Moore machine vs Mealy machine?
answer:  Moore: Output depends only on state.
 Mealy: Output depends on state and input.

questions.question: What is a multiplexer?
questions.answer: A digital switch that selects one input from many and forwards it to output.

questions.question: What is a priority encoder?
questions.answer: Encodes the input line with the highest priority.

questions.question: What is glitch in digital logic?
questions.answer: An unwanted transition due to gate delays causing incorrect output.

questions.question: What is cross talk?
questions.answer: Unwanted coupling between adjacent wires causing signal interference.

questions.question: What is yield in VLSI fabrication?
questions.answer: Percentage of functioning chips per wafer.

questions.question: What is the difference between Verilog and VHDL?
questions.answer: Verilog is more C-like, widely used in the US. VHDL is strongly typed, popular in Europe.

questions.question: What is RTL-to-GDSII flow?
questions.answer: Design process from coding to final layout – includes synthesis, P&R, DRC, LVS.

questions.question: What is physical design?
questions.answer: Converting netlist into layout (placement, routing, and verification).

questions.question: What is Design for Testability (DFT)?
questions.answer: Adding test logic to improve test coverage and ease debugging.

questions.question: What is latch-up in CMOS?
questions.answer: A short circuit due to parasitic transistors turning on, causing high current draw.

questions.question: What is dual-rail logic?
questions.answer: Logic using both true and complementary signals to reduce noise.

questions.question: What is race condition?
questions.answer: Undesired behavior when circuit output depends on the order of signal arrival.

questions.question: What is a PLA (Programmable Logic Array)?
questions.answer: A programmable device used to implement combinational logic.

questions.question: What is technology node?
questions.answer: Feature size (e.g., 7nm, 5nm) indicating the minimum transistor dimension.

questions.question: What is antenna effect in VLSI?
questions.answer: Charge accumulation during plasma etching that can damage transistors.

questions.question: What is GDSII file?
questions.answer: The final file format used to represent the layout of an IC for fabrication.

questions.question: What is the purpose of decoupling capacitors?
questions.answer: To suppress voltage fluctuations by stabilizing power supply lines.

questions.question: What is the role of a D flip-flop?
questions.answer: It stores and synchronizes data on the rising/falling edge of the clock.
